title: Sharoline Daters murder: Case continues next year
description: GEORGE NEWS - The case against Micaela Smith...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The case against Micaela Smith (24), the woman accused of Sharoline Daters' (26) murder in March, briefly appeared in the Thembalethu Magistrate's Court on Friday morning, 14 November.


> According to the Southern Cape Police's spokesperson, Sergeant Chris Spies, the Conville Police opened a murder docket following the incident.


Unconfirmed reports suggest that Smith is Daters' cousin's ex-girlfriend, and that she has two children with him.

Daters was reportedly in the company of her cousin in Protea Park in the early hours of Sunday 9 March when a dispute allegedly broke out between him and Smith regarding their children.

Daters sustained a stab wound to the chest and died in hospital later.

Smith was arrested shortly after the incident and released on bail after her first court appearance.


> Sharoline's father, Jacob (65), told George Herald shortly after her death that he was struggling to come to terms with the loss of his only daughter.


He described Sharoline as a gentle soul with a beautiful smile who cared for him deeply, someone always ready to help. "She was a loving person and looked after me so well. I cannot understand why anyone would want to take her life. Now I stand here alone, cooking for myself."

The matter has been postponed to 27 January for plea and trial.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
